name: Focaccia (Italian Flatbread)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 1 1/2 cups of flour, rosemary, salt, and yeast.
2. Add the oil and hot water. Stir until well combined.
3. Mix for 30 seconds on low speed, scraping the sides of the bowl frequently.
4. Increase speed to medium and stir for an additional minute, continuing to scrape the bowl.
5. Add enough remaining flour to make a dough that is easy to handle. Turn onto a lightly floured surface.
6. Knead for 5 to 8 minutes or until smooth and elastic.
7. Place in a greased bowl, with the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size (the dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched).
8. Punch down the dough. Brush a 12-inch pizza pan or large cookie sheet with oil.
9. Press the dough into the pan or flatten it into a 12-inch circle on the cookie sheet.
10. Make indentations, about 2 inches apart, using your fingers.
11. Brush the dough with oil and sprinkle with pepper.
12. Let rise uncovered in a warm place for an additional 30 minutes.
13. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
14. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown, brushing with additional oil before serving.
If using quick-acting yeast, omit the first rise. After kneading, cover dough and let rest for 10 minutes. Press into the pan and continue as directed. For high altitude locations (3500 to 6500 feet), rising times may be slightly shorter.
